name: Herb-Seasoned Stuffing
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a skillet over medium heat.
2. Add thyme, sage, rosemary, and dried lemon rind to the melted butter. Stir until well combined.
3. Add the bread cubes to the mixture. Gently toss or stir until the bread is evenly coated with the herb mixture.
4. Spread the seasoned bread cubes on a baking sheet in a single layer. Allow them to dry by air at room temperature, or dry in a very slow oven set to its lowest setting for about 1 hour.
5. Once dried, these bread cubes can be used as croutons or rehydrated with salt and water when ready to use as stuffing.
6. Yield: Approximately 8 cups (serving size: 1/2 cup). Serving exchange: 1 bread serving; 1 fat serving. Calorie count: 75 calories per serving. Converted by MMCONV, version 1.10.
